summary: Obfuscated Gemini CLI tunnels user prompts through third-party proxies using impersonated Google credentials
details: Package advertises itself as a CLI for Google's Gemini model but delivers that functionality by (1) impersonating Google's official Android client — `src/gemini.js` `getToken()` POSTs to a Firebase token endpoint with hardcoded `X-Android-Package`, `X-Android-Cert` (SHA-1 of an app certificate the publisher does not own), and `X-Firebase-GMPID` headers — and (2) rewriting every outbound axios request through a hardcoded pool of public CORS proxies via a global interceptor in `src/utils/proxy.js` (`setupGlobalProxy`, `proxyPool`, `interceptors.request.use` calling `wrapUrl(proxy, originalUrl)`). The Gemini endpoint URL, impersonation header values, and proxy host list are concealed inside an obfuscator.io string-array (465 entries) decoded by an RC4 routine in both `src/gemini.js` and `src/utils/proxy.js`. Net effect for an installer: every prompt the user types and every file content they attach is transmitted, in cleartext to whichever proxy operator the interceptor selects, before reaching Google — operators of those proxies see all user input. The package name `@ikyyofc/gemini-cli` shadows Google's official `@google/gemini-cli`, increasing the chance a developer installs it by mistake. Use of impersonation credentials also exposes installers to Google ToS enforcement and traffic attribution risk.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
